channel.title: Posts on My Learning Reflection Journal
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on My Learning Reflection Journal

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Hello! I’m Nowman

I’m a student at Azim Premji University studying Information Science. This blog is my reflection journal for documenting my learning journey.

About Me

Name: Nowman Ahamed

Course: Information Science

University: Azim Premji University

Student ID: BSIS1250034

GitHub: nowman-ug25 (https://github.com/nowman-ug25)

My Academic Interests

Information systems and databases

Web development and programming

Data analysis and visualization

Digital literacy and ethics

Purpose of This Blog This space will serve as:

item.title: My First Reflection
item.description:
INTRODUCTION

Information Science sits at the fascinating intersection of people, technology, and data. As I begin this course, I see it as the study of how information is created, organized, managed, and used—a discipline that shapes everything from library databases to the algorithms that power our daily digital interactions. This journal will track my journey into understanding these complex systems

-–

title: “Unit 2: Reflections on Information Organization”

date: 2026-03-18T10:00:00+05:30

draft: false

-–

Unit 2: Making Sense of Information Organization

Key Learnings

Unit 2 focused on how information is structured to make it findable and usable. We covered metadata, classification systems (like taxonomies and folksonomies), and the principles behind organizing digital and physical information. I learned that good organization is invisible—when it works, we don’t notice it; when it fails, chaos ensues.
